title: Stories from Asgard: Norse Myths
titleZh: 阿斯加德的故事：北欧神话

q.en: Where did the Norse people live between 700 and 1100 CE?
q.zh: 公元700年至1100年间，北欧人居住在哪里？

options.en: Modern-day Italy according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 如今的意大利（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Southern Greece
options.zh: 希腊南部

options.en: Scandinavia, an area of northern Europe
options.zh: 斯堪的纳维亚，北欧的一个地区

options.en: Ancient Egypt
options.zh: 古埃及

explain.en: The Introduction states the Norse lived in Scandinavia, an area of northern Europe, where Vikings later raided from.
explain.zh: 引言部分说明北欧人居住在斯堪的纳维亚（北欧地区），维京人后来也从那里出征劫掠。

q.en: What did Odin give up in exchange for a drink from Mimir's well?
q.zh: 奥丁为了喝一口米尔姆井的水，放弃了什么？

options.en: One of his eyes
options.zh: 他的一只眼睛

options.en: His magical hammer
options.zh: 他的魔法锤

options.en: His two ravens
options.zh: 他的两只乌鸦

options.en: His throne in Asgard
options.zh: 他在阿斯加德的王座

explain.en: Mimir demanded one of Odin's eyes for a drink; Odin agreed and left an eye at the bottom of the well.
explain.zh: 米尔姆要求奥丁用一只眼睛来换一口井水；奥丁答应并把一只眼睛留在了井底。

q.en: In the phrase 'Thor's signature weapon,' what does 'signature' most likely mean?
q.zh: 在短语“索尔的招牌武器”中，“signature”最可能是什么意思？

options.en: A legal contract
options.zh: 一份法律合同

options.en: Something special that identifies or sets apart a person
options.zh: 能表明身份或区别于他人的标志性事物

options.en: A written name on a document according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 文件上手写的名字（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A secret password
options.zh: 一个秘密口令

explain.en: The Glossary defines 'signature' as a special trait or mark that sets apart or identifies someone or something.
explain.zh: 词汇表将“signature”定义为能区分或标识某人/某物的特殊特征或标志。

q.en: According to the myth, what was wrong with Thor's hammer?
q.zh: 根据神话，索尔的锤子有什么缺陷？

options.en: It broke after one strike
options.zh: 它一击之后就碎裂了

options.en: Its handle was slightly too short
options.zh: 它的手柄略微偏短

options.en: It was made entirely of ice
options.zh: 它完全由冰制成

options.en: It could not return to its master
options.zh: 它无法飞回主人手中

explain.en: Because Loki stung the dwarf's eyelid, the hammer came out with one imperfection: its handle was slightly too short.
explain.zh: 因为洛基蛰了矮人的眼皮，锤子出炉时有一个瑕疵：手柄做得略微偏短。

q.en: What do the three myths in this book have in common?
q.zh: 书中这三个神话有什么共同点？

options.en: They are all about human history textbooks
options.zh: 它们都是关于人类历史课本

options.en: They all show conflict or trickery involving gods and giants
options.zh: 它们都展现了神与巨人之间的冲突或诡计

options.en: They describe the exact same single event according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 它们描述的是完全相同的同一件事（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: They all take place in modern Italy
options.zh: 它们都发生在现代的意大利

explain.en: Each myth features gods (Odin, Thor, Loki) dealing with giants or trickery, fitting the book's theme of god-versus-giant conflict.
explain.zh: 每个神话都涉及神（奥丁、索尔、洛基）与巨人或诡计周旋，契合书中神与巨人对抗的主题。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing this book?
q.zh: 作者写这本书的主要目的什么？

options.en: To persuade readers to become Vikings
options.zh: 劝说读者去当维京人

options.en: To argue that giants are real animals according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 论证巨人是一种真实存在的动物（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To teach readers how to forge metal
options.zh: 教读者如何锻造金属

options.en: To retell well-known Norse myths and explain their common elements
options.zh: 复述著名的北欧神话并解释它们的共同元素

explain.en: The book presents three myths with an Introduction and Conclusion noting common elements, showing its purpose is to retell and explain.
explain.zh: 书中以引言和结语呈现三个神话并指出其共同元素，说明目的是复述并解释这些神话。

q.en: How is this book mainly organized?
q.zh: 这本书主要是如何组织的？

options.en: With an introduction, three myths, and a conclusion
options.zh: 包含引言、三个神话和结语

options.en: As a biography of one real person according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 作为某位真实人物的传记（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: As a dictionary of names
options.zh: 作为一本姓名词典

options.en: As one single long poem
options.zh: 作为一首长篇诗歌

explain.en: The Table of Contents lists an Introduction, Odin's Search for Wisdom, The Making of Thor's Hammer, Idun's Apples, and a Conclusion.
explain.zh: 目录列出了引言、奥丁寻求智慧、索尔之锤的锻造、伊敦的苹果以及结语。

q.en: After losing the bet, how did Loki avoid losing his head to the dwarves?
q.zh: 打赌失败后，洛基是如何保住脑袋不被矮人取走的？

options.en: He fought them using Thor's hammer according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他用索尔的锤子与他们战斗（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: He turned into a giant eagle
options.zh: 他变成了一只巨鹰

options.en: He simply ran away to Midgard
options.zh: 他直接逃到了米德加尔特

options.en: He argued they won his head but had no right to his neck
options.zh: 他争辩说矮人赢得了他的头，却无权取走他的脖子

explain.en: Loki pointed out the dwarves could take his head but not his neck, so they sewed his mouth shut instead.
explain.zh: 洛基指出矮人可以得到他的头，却无法不连脖子一起取走，于是矮人改为把他的嘴缝上作为惩罚。

q.en: How did the gods finally defeat the giant Thiassi?
q.zh: 众神最终是如何击败巨人夏西的？

options.en: They lit a fire as he flew toward Asgard and he burned
options.zh: 当巨鹰飞向阿斯加德时，他们点起大火将其烧死

options.en: Thor crushed him with the hammer
options.zh: 索尔用锤子把他砸碎

options.en: Odin gave him an extra eye
options.zh: 奥丁多给了他一只眼睛

options.en: They drowned him in Mimir's well according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他们把他淹死在米尔姆井里（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: As the eagle (Thiassi) pursued Loki to Asgard, the gods stacked logs and lit a fire; the eagle flew into it and was killed.
explain.zh: 当巨鹰（夏西）追逐洛基飞近阿斯加德时，众神堆起木柴点燃大火，巨鹰飞入火中烧死。

q.en: According to the Conclusion, how does Norse mythology view death and destruction?
q.zh: 根据结语，北欧神话如何看待死亡与毁灭？

options.en: As completely meaningless
options.zh: 视其为毫无意义

options.en: As always permanent and final
options.zh: 视其总是永久且不可逆转

options.en: As part of a cycle that allows life to begin anew
options.zh: 视其为循环的一部分，让生命得以重新开始

options.en: As something to be feared forever according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 视其为永远令人恐惧的事物（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The Conclusion says Norse mythology treats life as a cycle: death and destruction are opportunities to begin anew.
explain.zh: 结语说北欧神话把生命看作一个循环：死亡与毁灭是重新开始的契机。

prompt.en: The book says Norse myths often show a struggle between gods and giants, representing light versus darkness and winter. Can you think of another story you know where a struggle between two forces leads to renewal or a new beginning? How are they similar?
prompt.zh: 书中说北欧神话常展现神与巨人之间的斗争，象征光明与黑暗、冬天与生长的对抗。你能想到另一个故事，其中两种力量的斗争带来了重生或新的开始吗？它们有哪些相似之处？

en: Think about the cycle of the seasons, or stories where something must end before something new can grow.
Compare the role of the 'giants' (forces of winter and darkness) to a challenge or obstacle in the other story.
zh: 想想四季的循环，或者某些故事中旧事物必须先结束、新事物才能生长。
把“巨人”（冬天与黑暗的力量）与另一个故事里的挑战或障碍做对比。
